author: codex-root

patch.value:
## Finding families

- Integrity: missing endpoints, unregistered kinds, invalid payloads, duplicate canonical identity, self-links, inverse mismatch.
- Ordering: genuine cycles, stale blockers, contradictory blocks pairs, unreachable prerequisites, redundant transitive edges.
- Coverage: unexplained roots or isolates, stories without delivery links, bodies naming absent prerequisites, milestones without outcomes, work without evidence lineage.
- Temporal: stale inferred relationships, superseded endpoints, snapshot drift, history and materialized-view disagreement.
- Quality: generic related overuse, alias drift, dense cliques, hub inflation, low-confidence edges without evidence.

## Policy stance

Sparse is not automatically bad and dense is not automatically good. Every finding must state the violated policy, evidence, confidence, consequences, and safe next action. Waivers are explicit, attributable, scoped, reviewable, and time-aware.
Relationship governance and completeness audit: semantic edge validation, graph coverage profiles, isolates, stale links, and remediation plans
Add policy-aware graph quality checks and machine-executable remediation planning without assuming that every root, isolate, or sparse item is defective.

Audits validate registered kinds, endpoints, direction and inverse rules, payload schema, cardinality, canonical aliases, duplicate identity, provenance, and snapshot consistency; Cycle checks operate only over declared order-bearing kinds and remain separate from associative strongly connected component analysis; Coverage profiles vary by item type, lifecycle state, project profile, age, and role; roots, archives, decisions, reminders, and scratch work can be explicitly valid isolates; Reports include counts, rates, severity, deterministic bounded samples, cursors, change since baseline, and machine-readable issue and remediation codes; Dry-run plans propose exact add, remove, retype, supersede, waive, or investigate operations with confidence and source evidence; no auto-fix invents an edge; Active and terminal remediation are separated; closed-item changes require dedicated changelog-safe batches and immutable audit history; Health, validate, stats, aggregate, and package audit surfaces reuse one SDK policy and finding contract with token-efficient default output
Make relationship quality observable, enforceable, explainable, and safely remediable across active and terminal history
Maintainers can distinguish genuine graph debt from intentional sparsity and repair the former without fabricating context
Prevents dangling, contradictory, cyclic, stale, or meaningless relationship graphs from misleading agents and algorithms
Counts-first audits, bounded samples, profile-aware policies, waivers, dry-run plans, and resumable remediation workflows
Current validation covers basic dependency integrity but the all-status audit exposed isolates, edge-kind drift, shallow ordering chains, and body-to-edge mismatches

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate check 2026-07-13: existing validate,deps visualization,and one-time living-map audits do not provide schema-aware graph coverage profiles and remediation plans; this extends canonical governance rather than replacing those items.

message: Create relationship governance and completeness audit feature

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Manual relationship-density baseline (2026-07-14 pass #82) — the measurement this audit feature should automate. Method: dump .item.dependencies for every ACTIVE item + parent links; degree counts both endpoints; closed-item out-edges not counted (active-graph view). Before enrichment: 211 active items, 269 dep edges + 210 parent edges, 95 items with zero out-edges, 67 items with total degree <= 1 (parent-only). After a 67-edge related-kind enrichment batch: degree<=1 = 3 (pm-7zs0 reminder, pm-x2vx, pm-p5u6 — deliberate; no honest semantic partner). Enrichment structure worth encoding as audit heuristics: (1) scanner-finding clusters get a 2-level hub topology — members -> file-cluster representative -> rule-class representative (Semgrep non-literal-RegExp 30 items across 6 files; prototype-pollution 3; spawn-shell-true 4; dependabot-cooldown 2 under pm-oz8u); (2) story -> implementing-task edges (pm-6ixn -> pm-oz8u); (3) cross-epic complementarity edges documented in bodies but absent as edges (pm-n7rr <-> pm-usfg/pm-pbyu/pm-mpbb); (4) class-sibling edges from defect-family ledgers. Audit check candidates: degree<=1 ratio on active items, container out-degree zero, documented-in-body-but-unlinked pairs.

value.note: governance policies findings and remediation contract

value.text: Implementation progress: added a counts-first relationship governance report covering active and terminal dangling references, retired sentinels, ordering-only cycles, stale lifecycle blocks, isolates, sparse nodes, deterministic samples, explicit exemptions, cancellation, and safe remediation text; validation now reuses shared assembly.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Verification evidence: linked governance suite passed; full repository coverage passed 5,798 tests at exact 100/100/100/100; lint/static/security/package gates passed. Fresh packed SDK audit and CLI temp project proved dependency/lifecycle blocked parity, graph assembly, semantic traversal, bounded path evidence, and zero history drift. This tranche is complete; broader type/profile baselines, change-since-baseline, cursors, executable dry-run plans, and all audit-surface integration remain canonical open scope.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: PR #579 review round 1: integrity findings now repartition dangling references with the audit invocation's custom terminal predicate rather than assembly-time defaults. A custom terminal holder regression proves missing_reference_terminal versus missing_reference_active classification. Full 5,800-test coverage remains exact 100/100/100/100.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Continue: pm graph audit subcommand surfaces the governance audit engine on the CLI/MCP boundary

patch.value: pm graph audit subcommand: CLI/MCP adapter over auditWorkspaceRelationshipGraph with sample and exempt-isolate controls

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Implementation progress: the governance audit engine is now operable from the workspace surface via pm graph audit (CLI + pm_graph MCP + PmClient.graph). Counts-first envelope reports finding_count, findings_by_severity, findings_by_code (subject-count weighted), and the structural coverage profile; --sample bounds per-finding evidence, --exempt-isolate (repeatable/comma-separated) suppresses policy findings for deliberate isolates, --summary drops rows. Regression spec covers missing_reference_active + isolated_active_node detection, severity rollup consistency, and exemption behavior on a live temp workspace.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Remediation-surface addition via the graph-query-surface lane (2026-07-17): pm graph redundancy is the transitive-reduction detector this audit's stale/redundant-link remediation plans can consume — each finding carries the stored edge in its original spelling plus a semantic-orientation witness path, so remediation can propose removing shortcut edges with evidence. Live workspace scan found real blocked_by/blocks shortcut edges (bounded scan, truncated at --limit). Governance audit itself unchanged; redundancy stays a separate opt-in projection because shortcut edges are sometimes deliberate emphasis.
